steps.title: Identify the relationship being described
steps.content:
The passage explains that African languages made important contributions to Puerto Rican Spanish.

The sentence then gives a specific example: vowel sounds in Puerto Rican Spanish are being linked back to their source in Yoruba.

The blank needs a word that expresses this kind of origin or attribution relationship — how the Puerto Rican pronunciation connects to and comes from the African language.

steps.title: Match the word to the relationship
steps.content:
Traced is the only choice that expresses attribution to an origin.

The phrase “can be traced to” is a standard idiom meaning “can be attributed to” or “originates from” — exactly what the passage is establishing about Yoruba's influence on Puerto Rican vowel sounds.

The other three create nonsensical phrases: surrendered (give up), announced (make public), and offered (present) have no connection to linguistic influence or origin.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
